beginner.question: What are CSS container queries?
beginner.answer: Container queries allow you to apply styles based on a parent container's size rather than the viewport size, enabling truly responsive components.

beginner.question: What does container-type do?
beginner.answer: container-type establishes an element as a query container and specifies which dimension (inline-size, size, or normal) can be queried.

beginner.question: What does container-name do?
beginner.answer: container-name assigns a name to a container so it can be specifically targeted by @container queries.

beginner.question: How do you write a container query?
beginner.answer: Use @container (min-width: 400px) { .child { ... } } to apply styles when the container is at least 400px wide.

beginner.question: What are cqi units?
beginner.answer: cqi (container query inline) units are relative to the query container's inline size; 1cqi equals 1% of the container's inline dimension.

beginner.question: How do container queries differ from media queries?
beginner.answer: Container queries respond to a parent container's size; media queries respond to the viewport or device size.

beginner.question: What is the shorthand syntax for container?
beginner.answer: container: name / type; e.g., container: sidebar / inline-size; combines container-name and container-type.

beginner.question: Can you query container height with container queries?
beginner.answer: Yes, if container-type: size is set, you can query both width and height; with inline-size you can only query inline dimension.

beginner.question: What is container-type: size vs inline-size?
beginner.answer: size enables queries on both inline and block dimensions; inline-size enables queries only on the inline (typically width) dimension.

beginner.question: Do container queries work with all CSS properties?
beginner.answer: Container queries work with most properties inside the query block, but the container-type property itself is not queryable.

intermediate.question: How do you create a component that responds to its parent width?
intermediate.answer: Set container-type: inline-size on the parent, then use @container (min-width: value) to adjust child component styles.

intermediate.question: How do you use named containers with @container?
intermediate.answer: Set container-name: card on the container, then @container card (width > 300px) { ... } queries only that specific container.

intermediate.question: What happens when multiple containers are nested?
intermediate.answer: The nearest ancestor with a container-type responds to the query; container names disambiguate which container to query.

intermediate.question: How do container query units (cqi, cqw, cqh, cqmin, cqmax) work?
intermediate.answer: cqi = 1% of inline size, cqw = 1% of width, cqh = 1% of height, cqmin/cqmax = the smaller/larger of cqi and cqb.

intermediate.question: Can you combine container queries with media queries?
intermediate.answer: Yes, they are complementary; use media queries for page-level layout and container queries for component-level responsiveness.

intermediate.question: How do container queries affect performance?
intermediate.answer: Container queries can improve performance by avoiding viewport-wide recalculations, but each container query adds to the style recalc scope.

intermediate.question: How do you style a container element itself based on its own size?
intermediate.answer: You cannot query the container's own size directly; apply query styles to children and use a wrapper to style the container differently.

intermediate.question: What is the default value of container-type?
intermediate.answer: The default is normal, meaning the element is not a query container and cannot be queried by @container.

intermediate.question: How do container queries work with CSS Grid and subgrid?
intermediate.answer: Grid items can be query containers; subgrid elements inherit grid track sizes and can respond to container queries based on the subgrid's size.

intermediate.question: Can you use container queries inside @keyframes?
intermediate.answer: No, @container cannot be nested inside @keyframes; container queries are for style rules, not animation definitions.

advanced.question: How do you test container queries in browser DevTools?
advanced.answer: Chrome DevTools shows container badges in the Elements panel; right-click an element and inspect its container chain.

advanced.question: How do container queries interact with CSS cascade layers?
advanced.answer: Container queries respect cascade layer ordering; @container blocks within a higher-priority layer override lower-layer container queries.

advanced.question: How do you create a container query polyfill for older browsers?
advanced.answer: Use container-query-polyfill.js which observes element resize and applies query styles; fallback with CSS custom properties for progressive enhancement.

advanced.question: How do container queries interact with content-visibility?
advanced.answer: content-visibility: auto may skip rendering of offscreen containers, potentially delaying container query evaluation until the element is near the viewport.

advanced.question: How do you use container queries with CSS custom properties?
advanced.answer: Set custom properties on the container and use them inside @container blocks; the custom property values cascade into the query context.

advanced.question: How do container queries work with the :has() selector?
advanced.answer: :has() can be used within @container blocks to style elements based on their children's state, combining container queries with parent selection.

advanced.question: How do container queries affect server-side rendering (SSR)?
advanced.answer: SSR cannot evaluate container queries since container sizes depend on client rendering; use sensible default styles that upgrade on the client.

advanced.question: How do you use container query length units outside of @container rules?
advanced.answer: Container query units are invalid outside container queries and fall back to initial values or are ignored.

advanced.question: How do you handle container queries in iframe contexts?
advanced.answer: Containers inside iframes query their own parent container within the iframe, not the outer page's layout.

advanced.question: How do you implement container query-based typography scaling?
advanced.answer: Use cqi units on font-size: font-size: clamp(1rem, 3cqi, 2rem); typography scales fluidly with the container width.
